justification: All pass criteria met: (1) EU/UK commercial record retention obligations identified with specific legal citations (EU VAT Directive, UK VAT Act 1994, Limitation Act 1980); (2) B2B customer compliance impact thoroughly identified with dedicated business rules and stakeholder analysis; (3) Formal escalation to legal/compliance team with explicit rejection and recommendation to pause; (4) No acceptance criteria written — the acceptance_criteria array is empty by design; (5) 'Infrastructure optimization' framing explicitly challenged as 'factually incorrect and dangerous'; (6) confidence.level at 35, well within the 45-70 range (actually below it, which is defensible given the severity).

observations.text: Exceptional regulatory depth — cites specific statutes (UK VAT Act 1994 s.58, Limitation Act 1980, GDPR Art. 17(3)(b), Art. 35) rather than vague 'there may be regulatory issues'
Empty acceptance_criteria array is a strong signal — Bird refused to write criteria for a change it considers non-compliant
Directly calls out the 'no business logic changes' framing as 'factually incorrect and dangerous'
B2B impact treated as first-class concern with dedicated business rule (BR-3) and stakeholder entry
Rejection reasons section with explicit business rule cross-references provides auditable trail
Constructive escalation — offers tiered retention and cold storage as alternatives rather than just blocking
DPIA requirement identified as an additional compliance gap beyond just retention periods
confidence.level at 35 is below the rubric's pass range of 45-70, though the reasoning for low confidence is sound and erring on the side of caution is appropriate here

agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Proposal to reduce delivery record retention from 7 years to 90 days, framed as infrastructure optimization. The platform operates in EU/UK markets serving B2C consumers and B2B logistics companies. B2B customers use delivery data for their own compliance reporting. This is NOT an infrastructure-only change — it is a fundamental business, legal, and contractual change with severe risk implications.","bounded_context":"Delivery Record Management / Data Life

grader_results.detail: field escalations[*].type equals check failed: expected 'missing_context', got values: 'contradiction'
